name: Local Government and Panchayat Raj

sourceRefs.label: 2026_q page 108 Q128 (National Panchayat Raj Day=24 April; SC/ST reservation=73rd Amendment, with answer key)
2017_q1 page 20 Q136 (Constitutional status to PRIs initiated by L.M. Singhvi Committee, with answer key)
2025_q1 page 54 Q113 (TN Panchayats Act 1994 — Chapter II=Gram Sabha, Chapter XII=Penalties, with answer key)
2026_q page 27 Q26 (Article 243A/I/K/O matched to Gram Sabha/State Finance Commission/State Election Commission/Bar to court interference, with answer key)
vajiramandravi.com/clearias.com - Balwantray Mehta/Ashok Mehta/G.V.K.Rao Committees; 73rd Amendment timeline

researchNotes: 4 real hits, very precisely Article-numbered — TNPSC clearly expects exact Article-to-provision mapping for Part IX (Panchayats), not just broad concepts. Note a minor real-world nuance: some secondary sources credit the G.V.K. Rao Committee (1985) as the first to suggest constitutional status for PRIs, but the actual real exam question tested L.M. Singhvi Committee (1986) as the answer for 'initiated the recommendation for constitutional status' — going with the exam-confirmed answer as authoritative, while still covering all 4 committees since each has a genuinely distinct, separately-testable contribution.

reading.intro: Covers the evolution of Panchayati Raj through 4 key committees, and the 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992 (Part IX, Articles 243-243O) which gave Panchayats constitutional status. Real exam evidence is unusually precise about exact Article numbers and committee names — expect 'which committee recommended X' and 'which Article covers Y' formats.

qa.question: What did each of the four key Panchayati Raj committees specifically recommend — Balwantray Mehta, Ashok Mehta, G.V.K. Rao, and L.M. Singhvi?
qa.answer: Balwantray Mehta Committee (1957): recommended a 3-tier Panchayati Raj system (Gram Panchayat-village, Panchayat Samiti-block, Zila Parishad-district) — the original blueprint; Rajasthan adopted it first (1959). Ashok Mehta Committee (1977-78): recommended REPLACING the 3-tier with a 2-tier system (Zila Parishad + Mandal Panchayat) to revive a declining system — recommendation not widely adopted. G.V.K. Rao Committee (1985): reviewed rural development administration, called PRIs vital and recommended strengthening them. L.M. Singhvi Committee (1986): recommended PRIs be constitutionally recognised/entrenched, with the Gram Sabha as the base of decentralised democracy — this recommendation most directly led toward the 73rd Amendment.

qa.question: Real exam: Whose recommendation specifically initiated the move toward giving Panchayat Raj Institutions constitutional status?
qa.answer: The L.M. Singhvi Committee (1986) — confirmed by a real exam question, distinguishing it from the Ashok Mehta and Balwantray Mehta Committees offered as distractor options.

qa.question: What did the 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992 do, and when did it come into force?
qa.answer: It added Part IX (Articles 243-243O) to the Constitution, giving Panchayati Raj Institutions constitutional status — mandating a 3-tier system in every State, elections every 5 years, reservation of seats for SC/ST and women, and devolution of 29 subjects (Eleventh Schedule) to Panchayats. It received Presidential assent on 20 April 1993 and came into force on 24 April 1993 — commemorated since as National Panchayati Raj Day (24 April), confirmed by a real exam question.

qa.question: Real exam: Which Article of the Constitution introduced reservation of seats for SC/ST in Panchayats, and which Amendment is this part of?
qa.answer: Article 243D — part of the 73rd Constitutional Amendment, 1992 — confirmed by a real exam question.

qa.question: Real exam: Match Articles 243A, 243I, 243K, and 243O to their correct subjects.
qa.answer: Article 243A: Gram Sabha. Article 243I: constitution of the State Finance Commission (to review Panchayats' financial position). Article 243K: constitution of the State Election Commission (to conduct Panchayat elections). Article 243O: bar to interference by courts in Panchayat electoral matters — all four confirmed via a real exam's match-the-following question.

qa.question: What is the 74th Constitutional Amendment Act, and how does it relate to the 73rd?
qa.answer: Passed alongside the 73rd Amendment (1992), the 74th Amendment added Part IXA (Articles 243P-243ZG) to the Constitution, giving constitutional status to URBAN local bodies (Municipalities/Municipal Corporations) — the urban counterpart to the 73rd Amendment's rural Panchayats, with its own Twelfth Schedule of devolved subjects (distinct from Panchayats' Eleventh Schedule).

qa.question: Real exam: Which chapters of the Tamil Nadu Panchayats Act, 1994 deal with the Gram Sabha and Penalties, and which commonly-assumed pairing is actually FALSE?
qa.answer: Chapter II deals with the Gram Sabha; Chapter XII deals with Penalties — both confirmed TRUE by a real exam question. The same question tested 'Chapter VII deals with Taxation and Finance' as FALSE (Chapter VII covers a different subject in the actual Act) — a reminder not to assume sequential/intuitive chapter numbering matches reality.

questions.prompt: The Balwantray Mehta Committee (1957) recommended which structure for Panchayati Raj?
options: A 3-tier system: Gram Panchayat, Panchayat Samiti, Zila Parishad
A 4-tier system including a national Panchayat body

questions.prompt: Which Committee specifically initiated the recommendation for giving Panchayat Raj Institutions constitutional status?
The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act came into force on, which is now observed as National Panchayati Raj Day:
Reservation of seats for SC/ST in Panchayats was introduced by the 73rd Amendment under which Article?
Which Article of the Constitution establishes the Gram Sabha?
Which Article provides for the constitution of the State Election Commission to conduct Panchayat elections?
The 74th Constitutional Amendment Act gave constitutional status to:
questions.explanation: Municipalities (urban local bodies), via Part IXA.

questions.prompt: Which Chapter of the Tamil Nadu Panchayats Act, 1994 deals with the Gram Sabha?
Which Chapter of the Tamil Nadu Panchayats Act, 1994 deals with Penalties?
